mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW Z4 2.0i / 2.2i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Z4 2.0i/2.2i engine typically costs £1,500–£3,500 supply only depending on generation. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A used N46B20B engine from a specialist costs approximately £1,295.65 including VAT and delivery. Main dealer new engines are no longer available for these older engine families. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW Z4 2.0i / 2.2i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine your Z4 has. The M54B22 (2.2i, 2003–2005) is a straight-six that is 'really very reliable' and with proper maintenance can last over 350,000 km. The N46B20 (2.0i, 2005–2008) is generally reliable, but timing chain guide failure is a documented concern. Check the Era Map above for your specific engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW Z4 2.0i / 2.2i?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Timing chain guide failure on the N46 four-cylinder — causing cold-start rattle and risk of catastrophic engine damage, (2) DISA valve failure on the M54 — causing loss of torque and rattling from the intake, (3) Oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing on both engines, and (4) Cooling system failures — water pump, thermostat and expansion tank.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW Z4 2.0i / 2.2i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £3,000–£7,000 Z4, a £2,500 recon is 35–80% of the car's value. For a clean M54 2.2i example, replacement is almost always worth it given the engine's legendary durability and the Z4's strong following. For N46 2.0i examples with no chain history, evaluate the car's overall condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW Z4 2.0i / 2.2i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: 2003–2005 2.2i models have the M54B22 (straight-six, 170 hp); 2005–2008 2.0i models have the N46B20 (four-cylinder, 150 hp). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N46 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£800–£2,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain slip (£2,500–£5,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The N46 is an interference engine — if the chain slips, valves will hit pistons.

eraMap.title: BMW Z4 2.0i / 2.2i — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 2.2L straight-six, 170 hp — buttery smooth, very reliable; DISA & VANOS seals are main weak points
2.0L four-cylinder, 150 hp — lighter and more efficient; N42/N46 chain guide issues less common but documented

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Z4 2.0i/2.2i engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). Timing chain replacement on the N46 requires specialist attention.

cta.label: → Compare Z4 2.0i / 2.2i engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Guide Failure (N46 2.0i, 2005–2008)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or slapping noise from the front of the engine on cold start, metallic ticking, rough idling, loss of power, engine warning light

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The N46 four-cylinder engine has a documented issue with timing chain guides breaking. One owner reported: "I've seen the oil pickup get clogged up on more than one 2.0 N42 engine when the timing chain tensioner has been at fault and the guides have been breaking". If the chain slips, valves can hit pistons — this is an interference engine. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain inspected immediately. A £800–£2,000 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £2,500–£5,500 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/n46-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: DISA Valve Failure (M54 2.2i, 2003–2005)
problems.symptoms: Loss of low-end torque, loss of top-end power, rattling noise from the intake manifold, reduced fuel economy, hesitation on acceleration
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: The M54 engine's DISA (Differenzierte Sauganlage) valve can fail, causing a rattle from the inlet manifold and a noticeable loss of either low-end torque or top-end power. A broken DISA flap can send debris into the engine — it's a well-known weak point on the M54. If you experience a loss of performance or hear a rattle from the intake, have the DISA valve inspected. Rebuild kits are available, or the entire unit can be replaced.

failureLink.label: Read the full DISA valve failure guide → /failures/disa-valve-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Both Engines)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell from engine bay, visible oil residue around the engine, oil spots under the car, low oil level warning
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil spots or burning smell

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are "a frequent complaint among Z4 owners". Common leak points include the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket on both the N46 and M54 engines. On the M54, the oil filter housing gasket is a known weak point. If left unaddressed, oil leaks can lead to low oil levels and potential engine damage. If you notice a burning smell or oil spots, have the gaskets inspected promptly.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide → /failures/oil-leaks-n46-m54/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Cooling System Failures (Both Engines)
problems.symptoms: Overheating, coolant loss, steam from engine bay, temperature gauge rising, heater blowing cold
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if temperature rises

problems.recommendation: Cooling system failures are a significant concern on both engines. Common problems include water pump failure, cracked plastic housings, and expansion tank leaks. Overheating can lead to severe engine damage, so it's crucial to address any cooling issues promptly. Preventative replacement of the water pump and thermostat at 60,000–80,000 miles is strongly recommended.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means full replacement required
Catch early to prevent oil contamination and engine damage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Z4 2.0i / 2.2i? Check These First
buyIf: * M54-engined car (2003–2005 2.2i) — these straight-sixes are "really very reliable"
* Documented cooling system refresh (water pump, thermostat, expansion tank)
* Full service history with regular oil changes
* No timing chain rattle on cold start (N46 cars)
avoidIf: * N46-engined car (2005–2008 2.0i) with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* DISA valve rattle from the intake manifold with no documented fix (M54)
* Overheating history or cooling system issues
* Poor service history — BMW engines are reliable but need proper maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Z4 2.0i and 2.2i offer two very different driving experiences. The M54 2.2i is a buttery-smooth straight-six that delivers 170 hp with genuine character and "really very reliable" performance. The N46 2.0i is a lighter, more efficient four-cylinder that replaced the 2.2i in 2005. The M54's weaknesses are well-understood — DISA valve, VANOS seals, and oil leaks — and are manageable. The N46's timing chain guide failure is the more critical concern. Buy a well-maintained M54 if you want the classic six-cylinder experience. Buy on documented history, not on price alone.

cta.label: → Compare Z4 2.0i / 2.2i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW Z4 2.0i or 2.2i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or slapping noise from the front of the engine (timing chain — N46)
* DISA Valve — Any rattling from the intake manifold or loss of low-end torque? (M54)
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve cover and oil filter housing
* Cooling System — Check for coolant leaks; ask about water pump and thermostat history
* Service History — Including which engine (N46 or M54) and confirmed oil change intervals
* VANOS — Any hesitation or flat spots in the power band? (M54)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for DISA, VANOS or timing-related codes
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
